name: Monte La Copa Trail
name_original: Monte La Copa (SL-CV-M) — Circular desde Montanejos
description: Hike the Monte La Copa Trail, a moderate 3.9-mile (6.3 km) loop offering panoramic views and varied terrain, taking about 2 hours and 18 min
region_intro: Hiking around Montanejos offers diverse terrain shaped by the Mijares River, which carves through dramatic gorges and canyons. The region features lush Mediterranean forests, mountainous landscapes, and unique thermal springs. This area provides a variety of hiking trails suitable for exploring its natural features. Best hiking trails around Montanejos The most popular hiking route is Los Estrechos Trail, a 3.1 miles (5.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 22 minutes to complete. This path leads through impressive gorges and riverscapes sculpted by the Mijares River. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Bojera Path (SL-CV 105) — Circular to the Cova Negra, a difficult 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This route explores varied terrain, including the area around the Cova Negra. Local hikers also love the Monte La Copa Trail, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 19 minutes. Hiking around Montanejos is defined by its river canyons, thermal springs, and verdant forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Montanejos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 1,800 hikers have used komoot to explore Montanejos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Montanejos?
A: Montanejos offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 12 easy, 14 moderate, and 12 difficult options, allowing you to explore the region's river canyons, thermal springs, and verdant forests.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Montanejos?
A: Yes, Montanejos has several easy trails suitable for families. A notable option is the 4.2km 'El Sendero Familiar' (Family Trail) mentioned in local guides, which follows the Mijares River, passing the thermal springs and a section of the stunning Los Estrechos de Chillapájaros. You can also find 12 easy routes on komoot, perfect for a leisurely stroll.

Q: What are some of the natural attractions or landmarks I can see while hiking in Montanejos?
A: Montanejos is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore the dramatic gorges of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5646525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrechos de Chillapájaros&lt;/a&gt;, visit the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Baños&lt;/a&gt; thermal springs, or discover the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877864888/the-bojera-path-sl-cv-105-circular-to-the-cova-negra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Negra&lt;/a&gt;. The ruins of the Aljama Castle, offering panoramic views, are also accessible via trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e879101570/the-castle-path-sl-cv-104-circular-to-the-castle-of-montanejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Castle Path&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Montanejos?
A: Yes, many trails in Montanejos are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877864888/the-bojera-path-sl-cv-105-circular-to-the-cova-negra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Bojera Path (SL-CV 105)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e879101570/the-castle-path-sl-cv-104-circular-to-the-castle-of-montanejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Castle Path (SL-CV 104)&lt;/a&gt; are both excellent circular options that showcase different aspects of the region's landscape.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Montanejos?
A: Montanejos is generally dog-friendly, with many trails suitable for hiking with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in nature reserves or areas with wildlife, and to check specific trail regulations before you go. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Montanejos?
A: Montanejos is a great hiking destination year-round. The thermal springs maintain a constant 25°C, making them enjoyable even in cooler months. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for hiking through the lush forests and along the river. Summer can be warm, but the river and thermal springs provide a refreshing escape, while winter offers a quieter experience with mild conditions.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or natural pools along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely! The most famous spot for wild swimming is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Baños&lt;/a&gt;, where thermal waters maintain a constant 25°C. The Mijares River also offers numerous clear, refreshing spots along its course, particularly within the dramatic gorges, perfect for a dip after a hike.

Q: How do other hikers rate the trails in Montanejos?
A: The hiking trails in Montanejos are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning river canyons, the unique thermal springs, and the diverse landscapes that offer something for every ability level.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to impressive viewpoints?
A: Yes, Montanejos offers several trails with rewarding viewpoints. For instance, the 'Los Castillejos – El Morrón' trail, though challenging, leads to Morrón de Campos, the highest point, providing unrivaled panoramic views of the region. Additionally, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3484565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de Piedrahíta Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent spot for scenic vistas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in Montanejos?
A: The terrain in Montanejos is quite varied. You can expect paths that wind along the Mijares River through dramatic gorges and canyons, as well as trails that ascend into mountainous areas with pine, oak, and Mediterranean forests. Some routes feature impressive rock formations, while others lead to historical ruins, offering a mix of riverside strolls and more challenging ascents.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trailheads in Montanejos?
A: While Montanejos is primarily accessed by car, local bus services connect the town with larger nearby cities. However, direct public transport to specific trailheads outside the town center can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance or consider driving to access a wider range of hiking starting points.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is home to beautiful water features. While the most famous is the Arenoso Reservoir spillway, known as 'El Chorro,' you can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2931138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de la Novia of Cirat&lt;/a&gt; nearby, which is another stunning waterfall worth visiting. Many trails follow the Mijares River, offering picturesque cascades and river views.
tips: From its top at 846 meters above sea level you will have the best panoramic views of the town of Montanejos. It is an alternative route to the popular hot springs, ideal for the autumn and spring season, when temperatures are more pleasant and different colors are seen in the vegetation.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and typical duration of the Monte La Copa Trail?
faqs.answer: The Monte La Copa Trail is rated as moderate. It is a circular route that typically takes about 2 hours to complete, covering approximately 6.6 kilometers.

faqs.question: Is the Monte La Copa Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning it loops back to its starting point without requiring you to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Where does the Monte La Copa Trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically begins with a descent from a spa building towards a river. While specific parking details are not provided, trails of this nature often have parking facilities near the starting point in the vicinity of Montanejos. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Monte La Copa Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain. You'll encounter descents to a river crossing, ascents through terraced plots, sections along a road, and marked paths leading into a pine forest. It also includes meandering through abandoned terraced plots, passing rocky buttresses, and descending between boulders. Expect uneven surfaces and inclines.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints and landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the View of Montanejos from Mirador de La Copa, which offers panoramic vistas and interpretive information. Another notable point of interest is the "fuente de los Tres Hermanos" (spring of the Three Brothers).

faqs.question: Does the Monte La Copa Trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Monte del Copa trail (covering 99% of its length) and partially intersects with Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós (52%), La Conquista de Valencia (7%), Puente del Machón (5%), and Paseo Fuente de "Los Baños" (5%).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. However, if hiking during the summer, it's particularly important to carry sufficient water due to potential heat.

faqs.question: Is the Monte La Copa Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs are not detailed, many trails in natural areas allow dogs, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always best to check local signage at the trailhead for any specific restrictions regarding pets.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to hike the Monte La Copa Trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to hike this trail.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail passes by the "fuente de los Tres Hermanos" (spring of the Three Brothers), which can be a source of water. However, it's always recommended to carry your own water, especially on longer hikes or during warmer months.

faqs.question: What should I bring for hiking the Monte La Copa Trail?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring appropriate footwear (hiking boots or sturdy shoes), plenty of water (especially in summer), sun protection, and a map or GPS device. Snacks are also recommended for energy.

waypoints.name: View of Montanejos from Mirador de La Copa
